---
title: "Project Roadmap"
subtitle: "Timeline, Milestones, and Deliverables"
---

# Project Overview

**Project Start:** October 13, 2025  
**Preliminary Design Due:** December 18, 2025  
**Production Deployment:** March 2, 2026  
**Final Submission:** March 24, 2026  

This document outlines the complete project timeline for the Identity and Profile Management API thesis project, from initial pitch through final submission.

# Visual Timeline

```{mermaid}
gantt
    title Final Project - Personal Identity Service
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    
    section Completed
    Present the pitch           :done, pitch, 2025-10-13, 2025-11-04
    
    section Documentation
    Implementation Draft        :active, impl, 2025-11-05, 2025-11-28
    Preliminary Design          :prelim, 2025-11-29, 2025-12-18
    
    section Backend Development
    Phase 1: Auth + Security    :phase1, 2025-12-19, 2026-01-02
    Phase 2: Context Profiles   :phase2, 2026-01-03, 2026-01-12
    Phase 3: OAuth 2.1          :phase3, 2026-01-13, 2026-01-26
    Phase 4: Guardian + Privacy :phase4, 2026-01-27, 2026-02-09
    
    section Frontend
    Phase 5: Frontend           :phase5, 2026-02-10, 2026-02-23
    
    section Production
    Phase 6: Deployment         :phase6, 2026-02-24, 2026-03-02
    Phase 7: Testing            :phase7, 2026-03-03, 2026-03-09
    Phase 8: Refinement         :phase8, 2026-03-10, 2026-03-16
    
    section Final Submission
    Final Report + Video        :crit, final, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-24
    
    section Milestones
    Preliminary Design Due      :milestone, m1, 2025-12-18, 0d
    Production Live             :milestone, m2, 2026-03-02, 0d
    Code Freeze                 :milestone, m3, 2026-03-09, 0d
    Final Submission            :crit, milestone, m4, 2026-03-24, 0d
```

# Key Milestones

| Date | Milestone | Deliverable |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| Oct 13, 2025 | Project Start | Pitch presentation |
| Nov 4, 2025 | Pitch Complete | Video and initial documentation |
| **Dec 18, 2025** | **Preliminary Design** | Design document with architecture |
| Jan 2, 2026 | Authentication Complete | Users can register/login securely |
| Jan 12, 2026 | Context Profiles Complete | Multi-context identity working |
| Jan 26, 2026 | OAuth 2.1 Complete | Third-party authorization functional |
| Feb 9, 2026 | Backend Complete | All API features implemented |
| Feb 23, 2026 | Frontend Complete | Demo application ready |
| **Mar 2, 2026** | **Production Live** | System deployed to production |
| Mar 9, 2026 | Code Freeze | All testing and validation complete |
| **Mar 24, 2026** | **Final Submission** | Report and video delivered |

# Detailed Phase Breakdown

## Completed Work (Oct 13 - Dec 10, 2025)

| Task | Start | End | Status |
|------|-------|-----|--------|
| Present the pitch | Oct 13 | Nov 4 | DONE |
| Background Research | Oct 15 | Oct 31 | DONE |
| Problem Formulation | Oct 20 | Nov 5 | DONE |
| Literature Review | Oct 25 | Nov 15 | DONE |
| Personas and User Stories | Nov 1 | Nov 15 | DONE |
| Requirements Analysis | Nov 10 | Nov 25 | DONE |
| Architecture Design | Nov 15 | Dec 1 | DONE |
| Database Schema Design | Nov 20 | Dec 1 | DONE |
| Profile CRUD Implementation (TDD) | Dec 1 | Dec 10 | DONE |
| Test Infrastructure (89% coverage) | Dec 5 | Dec 10 | DONE |

## Current: Preliminary Design (Dec 10 - Dec 18, 2025)

| Task | Start | End | Status |
|------|-------|-----|--------|
| Comparison Matrix (existing solutions) | Dec 10 | Dec 14 | IN PROGRESS |
| Framework Justification Document | Dec 12 | Dec 15 | PENDING |
| Security/Crypto Design Preview | Dec 14 | Dec 16 | PENDING |
| Ethical/Legal Considerations | Dec 14 | Dec 16 | PENDING |
| **Preliminary Design Document** | Dec 18 | Dec 18 | DEADLINE |

**Deliverables for Dec 18:**

- Personas: Individual users (privacy-focused, professionals, minors) vs Organization use
- User Stories: 10-15 key stories with acceptance criteria
- Pain Points to Gains Matrix: What problems exist, how we solve them
- Comparison Matrix: Why Auth0, Okta, Keycloak fail to solve this problem
- Architecture Overview: Tech stack justification (FastAPI, PostgreSQL, etc.)
- Security Design Preview: Crypto choices (Argon2id, JWT RS256/HS256)
- Ethical/Legal Considerations: GDPR-inspired approach, privacy-by-design
- Project Plan: This Gantt chart with deliverables

## Phase 1: Authentication + Security (Dec 19 - Jan 2, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Status | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|--------|----------|
| Security Design (Argon2id, JWT, PKCE) | Dec 19 | Dec 23 | In Progress | 5 days |
| auth_users Table + Migration | Dec 21 | Dec 29 | **COMPLETED** | 8 days |
| Auth Service Implementation | Dec 23 | Dec 29 | Pending | 6 days |
| Auth Endpoints (register/login/logout) | Dec 27 | Dec 31 | Pending | 4 days |
| JWT Middleware | Dec 29 | Jan 1 | Pending | 3 days |
| Auth Tests (TDD) | Dec 19 | Jan 2 | Ongoing | ongoing |

**Security Design Decisions:**

- Password hashing: Argon2id (memory-hard, GPU-resistant)
- JWT signing: RS256 for production, HS256 for development
- Token lifecycle: Short-lived access tokens (15min), long-lived refresh tokens (7d)
- PKCE: Mandatory for all OAuth flows

**Completed Implementations:**

- **auth_users Table (Dec 29, 2025)**: Complete authentication credentials storage with 1:1 relationship to base_profiles. Includes email verification, login protection, password reset, and soft delete support. Tested with 5 sample users and Mailpit email testing integration.

## Phase 2: Context Profiles (Jan 3 - Jan 12, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| context_profiles Table + Migration | Jan 3 | Jan 5 | 3 days |
| Context Resolution Engine | Jan 5 | Jan 8 | 4 days |
| Profile Inheritance Logic | Jan 6 | Jan 10 | 4 days |
| Multilingual Fallback | Jan 9 | Jan 11 | 3 days |
| Context Tests (TDD) | Jan 3 | Jan 12 | ongoing |

**Key Features:**

- Context-specific identity presentations (professional, social, legal)
- Profile inheritance with field-level overrides
- Multilingual name resolution based on Accept-Language header
- Temporal versioning for identity evolution

## Phase 3: OAuth 2.1 Server (Jan 13 - Jan 26, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| OAuth Tables (clients, tokens, consents) | Jan 13 | Jan 16 | 4 days |
| Authorization Code Flow + PKCE | Jan 16 | Jan 20 | 5 days |
| Token Issuance and Validation | Jan 18 | Jan 22 | 4 days |
| Scope-Based Filtering | Jan 20 | Jan 24 | 4 days |
| Consent Management | Jan 22 | Jan 26 | 4 days |
| OAuth Tests (TDD) | Jan 13 | Jan 26 | ongoing |

**OAuth 2.1 Features:**

- Authorization Code Flow with mandatory PKCE
- Client registration and management
- Scope-based access control for selective disclosure
- Consent tracking with revocation support
- Refresh token rotation

## Phase 4: Guardian + Privacy + Audit (Jan 27 - Feb 9, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| guardian_relationships Table | Jan 27 | Jan 29 | 3 days |
| Guardian Permission System | Jan 29 | Feb 2 | 4 days |
| Audit Logging (immutable) | Feb 2 | Feb 5 | 4 days |
| Data Export (GDPR Art. 15) | Feb 5 | Feb 7 | 3 days |
| Soft Deletion + Retention | Feb 7 | Feb 9 | 3 days |
| Privacy Tests (TDD) | Jan 27 | Feb 9 | ongoing |

**Privacy-by-Design Features:**

- Guardian-minor relationship management with permission hierarchies
- Immutable audit logging for all identity operations
- Data export capability (Right of Access)
- Soft deletion with configurable retention period (Right to Erasure)
- Consent tracking and withdrawal

## Phase 5: Frontend + UI/UX (Feb 10 - Feb 23, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| UI/UX Design and Red Routes | Feb 10 | Feb 13 | 4 days |
| Wireframes and Mockups | Feb 11 | Feb 14 | 4 days |
| Vue App Setup | Feb 13 | Feb 15 | 3 days |
| Profile Management UI | Feb 15 | Feb 18 | 4 days |
| OAuth Consent Flow UI | Feb 17 | Feb 20 | 4 days |
| Guardian Dashboard | Feb 19 | Feb 22 | 4 days |
| Frontend Tests | Feb 20 | Feb 23 | 4 days |

**UI/UX Considerations:**

- Red routes identification (critical user journeys)
- Accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Responsive design for multiple devices
- Clear consent mechanisms with transparent data usage

## Phase 6: Deployment to Production (Feb 24 - Mar 2, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| Render.com Infrastructure Setup | Feb 24 | Feb 25 | 2 days |
| Supabase Production Database | Feb 24 | Feb 25 | 2 days |
| Environment Configuration | Feb 25 | Feb 26 | 2 days |
| CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions) | Feb 26 | Feb 27 | 2 days |
| SSL/TLS and Domain Setup | Feb 27 | Feb 28 | 2 days |
| Database Migration to Production | Feb 28 | Mar 1 | 2 days |
| Production Deployment | Mar 1 | Mar 2 | 2 days |
| **Production Live** | Mar 2 | Mar 2 | MILESTONE |

**Infrastructure:**

- Hosting: Render.com (auto-scaling)
- Database: Supabase (managed PostgreSQL)
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions
- Monitoring: Application-level logging and metrics

## Phase 7: Testing + Validation (Mar 3 - Mar 9, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| Integration Testing (E2E - Production) | Mar 3 | Mar 5 | 3 days |
| Security Testing (OWASP) | Mar 4 | Mar 6 | 3 days |
| Performance/Load Testing | Mar 5 | Mar 7 | 3 days |
| End-User Testing (5-10 users) | Mar 6 | Mar 8 | 3 days |
| Bug Fixes and Hotfixes | Mar 7 | Mar 9 | 3 days |
| **Code Freeze** | Mar 9 | Mar 9 | MILESTONE |

**Testing Strategy:**

- End-to-end integration tests in production environment
- Security testing against OWASP Top 10
- Performance benchmarking (target: p95 < 200ms)
- End-user testing with 5-10 real users
- Feedback collection and critical bug fixes

## Phase 8: Refinement (Mar 10 - Mar 16, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| User Feedback Integration | Mar 10 | Mar 12 | 3 days |
| Documentation Polish | Mar 11 | Mar 13 | 3 days |
| Performance Optimization | Mar 12 | Mar 14 | 3 days |
| Final Bug Fixes | Mar 14 | Mar 16 | 3 days |

## Phase 9: Final Report + Video (Mar 17 - Mar 24, 2026)

| Task | Start | End | Duration |
|------|-------|-----|----------|
| Critical Analysis and Limitations | Mar 17 | Mar 19 | 3 days |
| Security Evaluation | Mar 18 | Mar 20 | 3 days |
| Final Report Writing | Mar 19 | Mar 22 | 4 days |
| Report Review and Editing | Mar 21 | Mar 23 | 3 days |
| Video Presentation (10 min) | Mar 21 | Mar 23 | 3 days |
| Final Review | Mar 23 | Mar 24 | 2 days |
| **Final Submission** | Mar 24 | Mar 24 | DEADLINE |

# Feedback Integration

Based on preliminary feedback, this project addresses the following requirements:

| Feedback Point | How Addressed | Phase |
|----------------|---------------|-------|
| Primary user identification | Personas document (individual vs org) | Preliminary Design |
| Pain points and solutions | Requirements analysis with gains matrix | Preliminary Design |
| Why existing solutions fail | Comparison matrix with Auth0, Okta, Keycloak | Preliminary Design |
| Full stack + framework justification | Architecture document | Preliminary Design |
| Crypto algorithms for auth | Argon2id, JWT RS256/HS256, PKCE | Phase 1 |
| Ethical/legal issues | GDPR-inspired privacy-by-design | Phase 4 |
| UI/UX and Red Routes | Frontend design phase | Phase 5 |
| Sketches to testing plan | This roadmap document | Preliminary Design |
| End-user testing | Validation phase with 5-10 users | Phase 7 |

# Risk Mitigation

| Risk | Mitigation Strategy |
|------|---------------------|
| Timeline slippage | Buffer time built into refinement phase |
| Technical complexity | TDD approach ensures incremental progress |
| Production issues | Staging environment testing before production |
| User testing delays | Early recruitment of test users |
| Documentation gaps | Continuous documentation throughout development |

# Success Criteria

**Technical Excellence:**

- 80%+ code coverage with TDD approach
- Security best practices (OWASP compliance)
- Production deployment with monitoring
- Comprehensive API documentation

**Academic Requirements:**

- Strong requirements with research backing
- Solid security and privacy implementation
- Clear data model beyond simplest use cases
- Robust, widely applicable API
- Frontend demonstrating multiple use cases
